description_long: This object is a tall, rectangular stone block column composed of several interlocking, roughly hewn stone segments. The surface is dark gray with subtle variations in tone, and faint cracks and seams are visible between the individual stone blocks, giving it a weathered, ancient appearance. The base is wider and square, providing stability, while the shaft tapers slightly as it rises to a flat, square top. The top surface is broad and flat, suitable for supporting other objects or structures. The stone has a matte, rough finish, with visible chisel marks and irregularities that suggest it was hand-carved. There are no visible engravings or decorative elements, emphasizing its utilitarian function as a structural or barrier element.
description: A tall, dark gray stone column made of interlocking blocks with a rough, weathered surface and a broad, flat top. The base is square and sturdy, and the stone shows visible seams and chisel marks.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height of the column, with visible seams between stone blocks and a prominent square base and top.
description_view_1: Side view is nearly identical to the front, highlighting the same block arrangement and proportions.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, with the same block pattern and surface details visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the same block structure and weathered surface as the other sides.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or plaza as a decorative or structural barrier
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or mortared onto a stable foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: Minor surface cracks and slight discoloration suggest some weathering, but no significant erosion or moss growth is visible.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: stacked in a column, likely as part of a row or series of similar blocks
